deno_json_has_check_task.reason: deno.json contains 'check': 'deno run -A scripts/check.ts' which delegates to a script that runs fmt --check, lint, and test — comprehensive verification.

deno_json_has_test_task.reason: deno.json contains 'test': 'deno test -A' — runs the full test suite.

deno_json_has_dev_task.reason: deno.json contains 'dev': 'deno run --watch -A src/main.ts' — includes the --watch flag for watch mode.

deno_json_has_prod_task.reason: deno.json contains 'prod': 'deno run -A src/main.ts' — runs production mode without watch.

check_script_created.reason: scripts/check.ts was created and contains logic for: run('deno fmt --check', ...), run('deno lint', ...), run('deno test', ...) — all three verification steps are present.

check_script_exits_on_failure.reason: The script ends with 'if (f > 0) Deno.exit(1);' where f is the count of failed checks — exits with code 1 on any failure.

existing_config_preserved.reason: The original deno.json had 'compilerOptions': {'strict': true} and 'imports': {'@std/assert': 'jsr:@std/assert@^1.0.0'}. Both are present verbatim in the final deno.json alongside the newly added tas…

tasks_reference_scripts.reason: The 'check' task (the complex one) references 'deno run -A scripts/check.ts'. The other tasks (test, dev, prod) are simple single-command invocations, not complex inline logic, so delegating them to …

check_runs_parallel.reason: checks.ts creates all three run() promises in an array and passes them to Promise.all(checks) — all three checks start concurrently before any awaiting happens.

check_buffers_output.reason: Each Deno.Command is created with stdout: 'piped' and stderr: 'piped', and output is decoded only after proc.output() resolves — no inherited streams, no interleaving possible.

check_no_external_deps.reason: scripts/check.ts uses only Deno built-ins: Deno.Command, Deno.env, Deno.exit, TextDecoder. No import statements referencing external packages or npm/JSR dependencies.

check_realtime_progress.reason: The run() function logs '>>> ${name} started' immediately when called (before awaiting), and logs the PASS/FAIL label immediately after proc.output() resolves — giving per-command start and finish st…
